Article Title: ‘No plans to enter airline business:’ Adani Group
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Adani Group has denied any plans to enter the airline business. An Adani Enterprises Limited spokesperson said on Friday: “We would like to categorically deny the recent media reports and market speculation suggesting that Adani Enterprises is planning to launch an airline. These reports are entirely baseless and factually incorrect. Adani Enterprises is not evaluating any proposal to enter the airline business.”The statement comes amid govt being reportedly being uncomfortable with a country of the size and economic potential of India having a duopoly of airlines — IndiGo and Tata’s Air India group. Akasa is also coming up in a big way. However, the West Asia crisis coupled with other India-specific issues like closure of Pakistan airspace and the rupee’s relentless crash have made things tougher for airlines here than they are for carriers globally. Both IndiGo and AI Group have slashed flights due to escalating operational costs. Smaller players like SpiceJet are struggling to survive.In this context, India is considering ways to make it easier to launch new airlines. One of these steps reportedly were allowing the operators of India’s busiest airports — Delhi and Mumbai — to hold more than 10% stake in airlines. The GMR and Adani groups run these two hubs, respectively.Both these conglomerates have major presence in aviation that goes beyond just running airports as they have maintenance, repair overhaul (MRO) and other businesses.The Adani Group has tied up with Embraer to make-in-India regional jets. However, the Brazilian aerospace company has linked setting up and operationalising the proposed final assembly line (FAL) to getting a firm order of at least 200 jets. Dholera in Gujarat has been selected as the site for this proposed FAL.Billionaire Gautam Adani-led conglomerate is the biggest private airport operator in India (in terms of number of airports). It operates hubs in Mumbai, Navi Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Lucknow, Jaipur, Guwahati, Mangalore and Thiruvananthapuram.
Article Summary: The adani group has denied any plans to enter the airline business. The statement comes amid govt being reportedly being uncomfortable with a duopoly of airlines. Both indigo and ai group have slashed flights due to escalating operational costs. India is considering ways to make it easier to launch new airlines.

Article Title: 'No cases against peaceful protesters': List of govt assurances that ended Sonam Wangchuk's fast
Article Text:
Wangchuk announced the end of his fast after Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh met him at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram around midnight and conveyed the government's assurances.

NEW DELHI: Activist Sonam Wangchuk on Friday ended his 26-day indefinite hunger strike after receiving assurances from the Centre that key demands raised by students protesting over alleged examination irregularities would be addressed.In a post on X, Wangchuk said the government had assured that there would be a discussion in Parliament on accountability in the examination system, compensation for families of students who died by suicide following the recent NEET paper leak, and no cases against peaceful protesters."END OF HUNGER... BEGINNING OF ACCOUNTABILITY...!" Wangchuk wrote.He added, "Sonam Wangchuk ends his fast after 26 days upon getting assurance from the government and members of parliament of all political parties that the issue of accountability in the failing examination system will be discussed on the floors of the Indian Parliament.""The government also gave assurance that families of suicide victims in the recent NEET examination paper leak will be given adequate compensation and that there will be no cases registered against the peacefully protesting students and youths," he said.Wangchuk announced the end of his fast after Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh met him at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram around midnight and conveyed the government's assurances."Just now in the presence of Union Ministers JP Nadda, Dr Jitendra Singh and the senior leaders of Apex Body of Leh Ladakh, I finally broke my fast after 26 days," Wangchuk wrote.He said 65 Members of Parliament from different political parties had either visited him or signed letters urging him to end the fast."This was done after long negotiations on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country. I will explain the conditions in detail in a separate video very soon. Meanwhile, I urge you all to stay very vigilant about not allowing violence of any kind anywhere," he said.Prime Minister Narendra Modi welcomed Wangchuk's decision to end the fast. In a post on X, he said, "I urge Sonam ji to follow his daily routine as advised by the doctors and regain his weight as soon as possible.""I pray to the Almighty that Sonam ji remains healthy," the Prime Minister added.The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), which has been leading the protest at Jantar Mantar, welcomed Wangchuk's decision but said its agitation would continue until Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.Wangchuk joined the CJP-led protest on June 28 and began an indefinite hunger strike in support of demands for accountability over alleged irregularities in competitive examinations, reforms to the education system and Pradhan's resignation. During the fast, he lost around 11 kg and continued the protest from Medanta Hospital after being shifted there from Safdarjung Hospital following legal proceedings.
Article Summary: Wangchuk announced the end of his fast after union ministers jp nadda and jitendra singh met him at medanta hospital in gurugram. He said 65 members of parliament from different political parties had either visited him or signed letters urging him to end the fast. Wangchuk joined the cjp-led protest on june 28.

Article Title: 'Don't insult students' intelligence': Rahul Gandhi slams PM Modi's midnight video on paper leaks
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Leader of opposition in Lok Sabha, Rahul Gandhi early Friday attacked Prime Minister Narendra Modi accusing him of sidestepping the demand for Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan 's resignation over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak.Reacting to the Prime Minister's late-night video message on the issue, Rahul said PM Modi should not "insult the intelligence" of students and demanded Pradhan be sacked saying the PM's address failed to raise students' key concerns.In a post on X, Rahul wrote, "Mr Modi, our students are demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Don't insult their intelligence with this pathetic midnight video."Listing his demands, he said:"1. Sack Pradhan.2. Punish those who beat students.3. Apologise."The leader of oppostion's remarks came hours after Prime Minister Modi announced that the government would bring a Bill in Parliament next week providing for stringent punishment against those involved in examination paper leaks.In a self-recorded video shared on X late Thursday night, the Prime Minister said the proposed legislation, which includes provisions for Fast-Track Courts and stricter penalties, would be discussed by the Union Cabinet on Friday before being introduced in Parliament when the second week of the Monsoon Session begins on Monday."Friends, I know that paper leaks are not an ordinary matter. For lakhs of students and their parents, it is extremely painful," PM Modi said."Our most important responsibility was to ensure that a whole year for the students is not wasted. It was very important to conduct the exams immediately. The government used its full strength to arrange exams for nearly 22 lakh students in the shortest possible time. And just five or six days ago, on the 19th, the results were also announced," he said.The Prime Minister said the government was now moving towards stronger legal action."Today I have issued directions to the departments to set up Fast-Track Courts. The departments worked tirelessly and, late last night, provided me with a draft. This draft, which includes provisions for Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishments, will be discussed in the Cabinet tomorrow," he said.He added that after incorporating suggestions from Cabinet colleagues, the Bill would be finalised and introduced in Parliament next week."Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth. Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared," PM Modi said.The opposition has been demanding Pradhan's resignation and a detailed discussion on the alleged NEET-UG paper leak during the Monsoon Session, with the issue triggering protests both inside Parliament and on the streets.Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike on Friday at Medanta Hospital in the presence of Union ministers J P Nadda and Jitendra Singh, along with senior leaders of the Apex Body of Leh.In a post on X, Wangchuk said he decided to end the fast after prolonged discussions and in view of the possibility of violence."Just now in the presence of Union Ministers JP Nadda & Dr. Jitendra Singh and the senior leaders of Apex Body of Leh Ladakh I finally broke my fast after 26 days... This was done after a long negotiation on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country," he said."I will explain the conditions in detail in a separate video very soon. Meanwhile urge you all to stay very vigilant about not allowing violence of any kind anywhere," he added.Meanwhile, the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) has called for nationwide peaceful protests on Friday, reiterating its demand for Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the alleged examination irregularities.
Article Summary: Rahul gandhi accuses prime minister narendra modi of sidestepping the demand for union education minister dharmendra pradhan's resignation over the alleged neet-ug 2026 paper leak. Rahul said pm modi should not "insult the intelligence" of students.

Article Title: Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike; PM Modi assures more stricter actions on paper leaks- top developments
Article Text:
Sonam Wangchuk ends his hunger strike; PM Modi pushes for Bill for fast track courts against paper leaks

Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike

PM Modi pushes for tougher anti-paper leak law

NEW DELHI: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike early Friday after the Centre assured him of action on key demands related to the student protests, while Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that the government would bring a new Bill with stringent provisions against examination paper leaks in a late night video. The proposed legislation, which includes fast-track courts and tougher punishment for offenders, will be discussed by the Union Cabinet on Friday before being introduced in Parliament next week.Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his indefinite hunger strike shortly after midnight at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram in the presence of Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh. He said the decision followed prolonged negotiations with the government and was taken "in view of possible violence in the country.""Just now in the presence of Union Ministers JP Nadda & Dr Jitendra Singh and the senior leaders of Apex Body of Leh Ladakh I finally broke my fast after 26 days. Earlier 65 Members of Parliament from different political parties had visited or signed letters urging me to break the fast. This was done after a long negotiation on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country. Meanwhile I urge you all to stay very vigilant about not allowing violence of any kind anywhere," Wangchuk posted on X.Following Wangchuk's announcement, Prime Minister Narendra Modi wished him a speedy recovery, saying, "I urge Sonam ji to follow his daily routine as advised by the doctors and regain his weight as soon as possible. I pray to the Almighty that Sonam ji remains healthy."Read more: Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike after 26 days Meanwhile, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke expressed relief that Wangchuk ended his hunger strike but stated that the protest will continue. "We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days. Thank you, sir, for your extraordinary courage and sacrifice. By putting your own life on the line, you awakened the conscience of an entire nation. Your life is far too precious to this country. The Cockroach Janta Party’s peaceful protest at Jantar Mantar will continue until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns," he said on X.In a late-night video message, Prime Minister Modi announced that the government would introduce a Bill with stringent provisions against paper leaks, including fast-track courts and harsher punishment for offenders. He said the proposal would be discussed by the Union Cabinet on Friday and the government would try to get it passed in Parliament next week."The Cabinet will discuss the issue of paper leaks on Friday... On Monday, when the second week of the Monsoon Session of Parliament begins, a Bill will be brought with provisions for strong action against paper leaks and we will try to pass it as early as possible," PM Modi said.Calling paper leaks "very painful" for lakhs of students and their families, the Prime Minister said the government had acted swiftly after the alleged NEET-UG paper leak."The guilty have been caught. They are now in jail. Our most significant responsibility was to ensure that the students do not lose a year... By using all resources, the government has ensured that 22 lakh students could attend the re-test," he said.PM Modi added that he had directed authorities to establish fast-track courts to ensure swift justice and said the proposed legislation would be another step towards making the examination system "foolproof" and protecting students' interests.Read more: 'More strict actions to come against paper leaks' - PM Modi announces new Bill push
Article Summary: Sonam wangchuk ends his hunger strike; pm modi pushes for bill for fast track courts against paper leaks. Climate activist wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike early friday after the centre assured him of action on key demands related to the student protests.

Article Title: Won’t forget Indian military’s rescue of 12 Chinese crew members after ship fire: China's defence attache
Article Text:
Defence Attache to India, Rear Admiral You Jian thanked Indian military for rescuing 12 Chinese crew members last year

NEW DELHI: China’s newly appointed Defence Attache to India, Rear Admiral You Jian, has thanked the Indian military for rescuing 12 Chinese crew members from a cargo ship that caught fire last year, saying Beijing “will not forget” the operation."We will not forget that, last June, the Indian Military rescued 12 Chinese crew members from a cargo ship that caught fire and exploded off the coast of Kerala," You said while speaking at a reception marking the 99th anniversary of the founding of the Chinese People’s Liberation Army (PLA). The Indian Navy and the Indian Coast Guard had jointly carried out the rescue operation to save the lives of the Chinese crew.A senior Chinese military officer lauding the effort of the Indian military is aimed at improving the relations between the two countries. New Delhi and Beijing have engaged in multiple constructive and forward-looking discussions towards gradual normalisation after the 2020 LAC faceoff.The Chinese defence attache also called for closer communication and cooperation between the armed forces of the two countries. He also highlighted the role played by the armed forces of both countries in United Nations peacekeeping operations. “We need to remember that both the Chinese and Indian armed forces have made outstanding contributions and great sacrifices in UN peacekeeping operations.We have always believed that the common interests between our two countries far outweigh our differences,” You said.On deepening military engagement, he said, “We are willing to work with the Indian side to maintain communication through diplomatic and military channels, enhance mutual trust and promote cooperation through dialogue, and contribute to the well-being of the peoples of China and India.”
Article Summary: Defence attache to india, rear admiral you jian thanked indian military for rescuing 12 chinese crew members last year. The indian navy and the indian coast guard had jointly carried out the rescue operation to save the lives of the chinese crew.

Article Title: SC laments vanishing of joint family system
Article Text:
SC expressed concern about the vanishing of the joint family system

Expressing concern about the vanishing of the joint family system and parents in nuclear families not spending quality time with their kids and instead handing over gadgets to keep them busy, Supreme Court Thursday said that it would lead to "disaster" as the new generation would be unaware of family bonding and relationships.While analysing the reasons behind recent incidents of people killing spouses, including the case of Sonam Raghuvanshi, a bench of Justices M M Sundresh and P B Varale said that it was a reflection of changing society. It said that the present generation is more intelligent and informative, but it does not know how to deal with pressure. The court said that it is unfortunate that people nowadays rely on WhatsApp-circulated messages and treat them as knowledge. Agreeing with the court's concern, solicitor general Tushar Mehta said that it has become a social problem and trust in human relationships is diminishing and there is a lack of tolerance.Justice Sundresh said that he was recently in a restaurant and he saw that a family gave a phone to a kid in order to keep him busy while they were having food. "If parents are replaced by gadgets, then it is a disaster. It is going to happen more in future. Joint family is an asset, and people don't understand it. Happiness does not come with material possession but by human bonding and relationship. We are becoming like machines," he said.
Article Summary: Sc expressed concern about the vanishing of the joint family system. It said that the present generation is more intelligent and informative, but it does not know how to deal with pressure. The court said that it is unfortunate that people nowadays rely on whatsapp-circulated messages and treat them as knowledge.

Article Title: India invites Bangladesh PM to Brics Summit
Article Text:
India invites Bangladesh PM

DHAKA/NEW DELHI: As part of Delhi's broader approach to enhancing global collaboration and cooperation, India has invited PM Tarique Rahman to attend the 18th Brics Summit, which will focus on global cooperation, trade, and sustainable development. India will host the summit in New Delhi on September 12-13.Bangladesh state minister for foreign affairs Shama Obaed Islam said Thursday that the ministry had received the invitation from the Indian side and forwarded it to the Prime Minister's Office, without elaborating further.PM Narendra Modi had invited Tarique in Feb too, after he took office. Tarique has so far not appeared inclined to take the political risk of visiting India while former PM Sheikh Hasina remains on Indian soil, amid concerns about a domestic backlash. However, Bangladesh also aspires to become a Brics member and has already received support from China and Russia.Both Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin are expected to attend the summit.Tarique recently visited Malaysia and China, and also plans to travel to Japan and the US for the UN General Assembly later this year. An official confirmation of India's invitation is still awaited.India had also invited Bangladesh in 2016, the last time it hosted the Brics summit in person, as a Bimstec member for a Brics outreach session.External affairs minister S Jaishankar on Thursday met Bangladesh foreign minister Khalilur Rahman on the sidelines of the Asean Regional Forum Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Manila. An official in Dhaka said the two ministers exchanged greetings.Responding to a question on regional instability, Shama Obaed said Bangladesh, as a democratic state with a democratically elected govt, wanted peace and stability in the region. She added that Bangladesh expected India, as a large democratic country, to address its internal issues and move towards a solution.India's Brics chairship is guided by the theme "Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability", reflecting a people-centric approach articulated by Modi at the 2025 Rio Summit. Brics comprises Brazil, China, Egypt, Ethiopia, India, Indonesia, Iran, Russia, Saudi Arabia, South Africa and the UAE as members.The 17th Brics Summit was held on July 6-7, 2025, in Rio de Janeiro in Brazil.
Article Summary: India invites bangladesh pm tarique rahman to attend the 18th brics summit. India will host the summit in new delhi on september 12-13. Bangladesh also aspires to become a brics member and has already received support from china and russia.

Article Title: From fan to critic: Dharmendra Pradhan’s 2023 post resurfaces, reveals changing equations
Article Text:
A 2023 photo of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and Sonam Wangchuk resurfaced recently (Picture credits: X).

Three years are a long time in public life. A 2023 photo of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan with education reformer Sonam Wangchuk and his wife Gitanjali Angmo has acquired a new political life.The March 2023 post, in which Pradhan praised the couple's ideas and commitment to transforming education, resurfaced on social media Thursday as Wangchuk's indefinite hunger strike over examination irregularities entered its 26th day.Wangchuk had responded with similar warmth, thanking Pradhan for being open to innovative ideas and saying the interaction had left them "even more committed to making NEP a reality on the ground". The contrast is sharp.Wangchuk, who had expressed enthusiasm about implementing NEP, is now the most prominent face associated with CJP 's agitation seeking accountability over NEET-UG paper leak and Pradhan's exit.
Article Summary: The march 2023 post, in which pradhan praised the couple's ideas and commitment to transforming education, resurfaced on social media thursday. Wangchuk is now the most prominent face associated with cjp's agitation seeking accountability over neet-ug paper leak.

Article Title: CJP's Dipke: Sonam Wangchuk's Hunger Strike Ends, Protest Continues Until Pradhan Resigns
Article Text:
"We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days. Thank you, sir, for your extraordinary courage and sacrifice. By putting your own life on the line, you awakened the conscience of an entire nation. Your life is far too precious to this country,"

"The Cockroach Janta Party's peaceful protest at Jantar Mantar will continue until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns,"

Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike

NEW DELHI: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke on Friday expressed relief after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike, while making it clear that its peaceful agitation at Delhi's Jantar Mantar would continue until Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.In a post on X, Dipke thanked Wangchuk for what he described as an "extraordinary courage and sacrifice" during the fast.Dipke wrote.However, he further said the end of Wangchuk's fast would not mark the end of the movement.Dipke said.Wangchuk had been on an indefinite hunger strike over alleged irregularities in the NEET examination and the government's handling of the issue.Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his indefinite hunger strike shortly after midnight at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram in the presence of Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh.He said the decision followed prolonged negotiations with the government and was taken "in view of possible violence in the country.""Just now in the presence of Union Ministers JP Nadda & Dr Jitendra Singh and the senior leaders of Apex Body of Leh Ladakh I finally broke my fast after 26 days. Earlier 65 Members of Parliament from different political parties had visited or signed letters urging me to break the fast. This was done after a long negotiation on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country. Meanwhile I urge you all to stay very vigilant about not allowing violence of any kind anywhere," Wangchuk posted on X.The CJP protest intensified on July 20 when the 'Sansad Chalo' march towards Parliament ended in clashes between protesters and Delhi Police after demonstrators allegedly attempted to breach barricades. The agitation has since continued at Jantar Mantar demanding the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan.The latest security measures, including the temporary suspension of mobile internet services and the early closure of commercial establishments in Connaught Place, come as authorities remain on alert amid concerns over further escalation around the protest site and adjoining parts of Central Delhi.
Article Summary: Cockroach janta party founder abhijeet dipke expressed relief after activist sonam wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike. Dipke thanked wangchuk for what he described as an "extraordinary courage and sacrifice" during the fast. Wangchuk had been on an indefinite hunger strike over alleged irregularities in the neet examination.

Article Title: India blasts Pakistan for raising J&K, Indus issues at Asean meet
Article Text:
Jaishankar slams Pakistan (File photo) Jaishankar slams Pakistan (File photo) Jaishankar slams Pakistan (File photo) 1

2

NEW DELHI: India slammed Pakistan for raising issues related to J&K and the Indus Waters Treaty at the Asean Regional Forum, saying the remarks by Pakistani foreign minister Ishaq Dar were baseless and unwarranted.The govt said in a statement it was no surprise that a multilateral forum had once again been cynically exploited by Pakistan to amplify falsehoods, peddle state-sponsored disinformation, and divert attention from its own well-documented record of sponsoring cross-border terrorism.External affairs minister S Jaishankar had said at the same forum that acts of terrorism will have consequences. "UT of J&K and Ladakh have been, are and will always remain integral and inalienable parts of India. Pakistan has no locus standi to comment on the same," MEA spokesman Randhir Jaiswal said.The govt said India's position on Indus Waters Treaty remained consistent. "IWT stands in abeyance in response to Pakistan's continued sponsorship of cross-border terrorism, including the Pahalgam attack. IWT will continue to be held in abeyance until Pakistan credibly and irrevocably abjures its support for cross-border terrorism," the official said.The govt strongly condemned Pakistan's continued practice of propagating officially sponsored disinformation, often dressed in religious terminology such as the use of terms like 'Fitna al-Hindustan'."This is a futile attempt by the Pakistani establishment to deflect attention from its own internal crises and its well-proven role as a safe haven for global terrorist networks. Instead of pointing fingers at its neighbours and misusing international platforms, Pakistan would serve itself and its people far better by dismantling the infrastructure of terrorism on its soil and putting its own house in order," the official said.
Article Summary: India slammed pakistan for raising issues related to j&k and the indus waters treaty at the asean regional forum. External affairs minister s jaishankar had said at the same forum that acts of terrorism will have consequences. "ut of j&k and ladakh have been, are and will always remain integral and inalienable parts of india," the govt said.

Article Title: India slams Pakistan's 'state-sponsored' misinformation at Asean, says Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance
Article Text:
India rejects Pakistan's ASEAN forum remarks​

NEW DELHI: India on Thursday categorically rejected Pakistan 's comments at the Asean Regional Forum in Manila, calling them "baseless and unwarranted".New Delhi slammed Islamabad of using the multilateral platform to peddle "state-sponsored disinformation" and divert attention from its record of sponsoring cross-border terrorism."It is no surprise that a multilateral forum has once again been cynically exploited by Pakistan to amplify falsehoods, peddle state-sponsored disinformation, and divert attention from its own well-documented record of sponsoring cross-border terrorism," MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said."The Union Territories of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh have been, are and will always remain integral and inalienable parts of India. Pakistan has no locus standi to comment on the same," MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said in response to media queries.Jaiswal also said India's position on the Indus Waters Treaty remained consistent, stating that the treaty stands in abeyance in response to Pakistan's continued sponsorship of cross-border terrorism, including the Pahalgam attack. "IWT will continue to be held in abeyance until Pakistan credibly and irrevocably abjures its support for cross-border terrorism," he said.Jaiswal also condemned Pakistan's practice of propagating "officially sponsored disinformation, often dressed in religious terminology such as the use of terms like 'Fitna al-Hindustan'.""This is a futile attempt by the Pakistani establishment to deflect attention from its own internal crises and its well-proven role as a safe haven for global terrorist networks," he said.He added that Pakistan would serve itself better by "dismantling the infrastructure of terrorism on its soil and putting its own house in order" instead of pointing fingers at its neighbours.External affairs minister S Jaishankar attended the foreign minister-level meetings under the Asean framework, including the Asean-India, East Asia Summit and Asean Regional Forum meetings in Manila over the last two days.Pakistan’s deputy prime minister and foreign minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar led Pakistan's delegation at the forum. He raised the Jammu and Kashmir dispute, calling for its resolution in accordance with UN Security Council resolutions, and expressed concern over India's decision to hold the Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance.
Article Summary: India categorically rejected pakistan's comments at the asean regional forum in manila. New delhi slammed islamabad of using the multilateral platform to peddle "state-sponsored disinformation" and divert attention from its record of sponsoring cross-border terrorism. India's position on the indus waters treaty remained consistent.

Article Title: Evening news wrap: Parliament deadlock over NEET continues, Gulf invests to find new oil routes & more
Article Text:
Evening news wrap

Government reaches out to opposition for NEET debate without preconditions; opposition insists on Pradhan's resignation.

Gulf nations spend billions on new oil pipelines to bypass Strait of Hormuz as Iran blockade continues.

CJP and government locked in standoff over venue for talks as protest demanding Pradhan's resignation continues.

India's first hydrogen-powered train clocks 900 km in five days of commercial operations on Jind-Sonipat route.

Iran claims its strikes destroyed Patriot systems and THAAD radar at US bases in Jordan and Kuwait; US yet to confirm.

Here are the top 5 stories of the day

Government reaches out to opposition for NEET debate, urges talks without preconditions

Gulf nations spend billions on finding new oil routes to bypass Strait of Hormuz

CJP protest continues as government, activists locked in standoff over venue for talks

Iran claims strikes destroyed Patriot systems, THAAD radar at US bases

India's first hydrogen-powered train clocks 900 km in five days

The government has reached out to opposition parties to break the Parliament deadlock over the NEET paper leak, offering a discussion without preconditions but opposing the demand for education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Union home minister Amit Shah met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla after repeated adjournments, while opposition leaders including Rahul Gandhi and Akhilesh Yadav also held talks with the Speaker. Read full story.

Gulf nations are accelerating pipeline projects worth billions of dollars to create alternative export routes bypassing the Strait of Hormuz, with at least seven major projects under construction, planned or under discussion. Saudi Arabia's East-West pipeline and UAE's Fujairah terminal are operating near full capacity, while Abu Dhabi is speeding up a $3 billion pipeline to increase Fujairah's capacity by 1.2 million barrels a day. Iraq is reviving plans for pipelines through Turkey and Jordan, but analysts warn alternative routes remain vulnerable to Houthi attacks. Read full story. The Cockroach Janata Party and the government remained locked in a standoff over the venue for talks, with the CJP insisting on Jantar Mantar while the government proposed an alternative location.The protest demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET paper leak has entered its second month. Police action against protesters and the detention of CJP leaders have further escalated tensions. Follow top developments. Iran's IRGC claimed it destroyed Patriot air defence systems, a THAAD missile defence radar and an MQ-9 Reaper drone depot in retaliatory strikes on US bases in Jordan and Kuwait, though the claims could not be independently verified and the US military has not confirmed any damage. Read full story. India's first hydrogen fuel cell-powered passenger train has covered around 900 kilometres during the first five days of commercial operations on the Jind-Sonipat route in Haryana, with passenger fares ranging between Rs 10 and Rs 25. Read full story.
Article Summary: Government reaches out to opposition for neet debate without preconditions. Cjp and government locked in standoff over venue for talks as protest demanding pradhan's resignation continues. India's first hydrogen-powered train clocks 900 km in five days of commercial operations on jind-sonipat route.

Article Title: Trump says Saudi nuclear deal hinges on kingdom joining Abraham Accords with Israel
Article Text:
Poll Vote & Share your view Should Saudi Arabia be required to normalize relations with Israel before receiving nuclear cooperation from the US? Yes, it's a necessary step. No, they should be treated independently. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

US President Donald Trump on Thursday said the proposed US-Saudi civil nuclear agreement would only be approved if Saudi Arabia normalises diplomatic relations with Israel by joining the Abraham Accords, adding that the deal would not permit uranium enrichment or any military nuclear programme.Trump's remarks come a day after the United States and Saudi Arabia signed a civil nuclear cooperation framework, known as a 123 Agreement, that will now require approval from the US Congress.In a post on social media, Trump said, "The Civil Nuclear Deal (There will be no enrichment of material!) being made between the United States Department of Energy and Saudi Arabia, which pertains only to non-military use such as the ones that Iran and UAE (and others) already have, will be approved, but is totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the very respected and successful Abraham Accords. The United States is not opposed to Civil (Non-Enriched) Nuclear Facilities. "Trump's statement marks a shift from the framework announced on Wednesday, under which the two countries agreed to cooperate on Saudi Arabia's civilian nuclear programme. The agreement, signed by US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Saudi Energy Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman, lays the groundwork for nuclear cooperation while requiring congressional review before it can take effect.The Trump administration has said the agreement adheres to the highest standards of nuclear safety and non-proliferation. Secretary of State Marco Rubio also defended the deal, saying the United States would not enter into any arrangement that increased the risk of nuclear weapons proliferation.The agreement has nevertheless drawn scrutiny from some US lawmakers, who have raised concerns that allowing Saudi Arabia to pursue uranium enrichment for civilian purposes could eventually create pathways for nuclear weapons development.Trump's latest remarks also appear to narrow the scope of the proposed agreement by insisting there would be "no enrichment of material" and linking final approval to Saudi Arabia's recognition of Israel through the Abraham Accords, the US-brokered normalisation agreements signed by Israel with several Arab states during Trump's first term.The announcement comes as the United States and Israel continue military operations against Iran, with Washington maintaining that Tehran's uranium enrichment programme could enable it to develop nuclear weapons, a charge Iran denies, insisting its nuclear activities are solely for peaceful purposes.
Article Summary: Us president donald trump said the proposed us-saudi civil nuclear agreement would only be approved if saudi arabia normalises diplomatic relations with israel. Trump's remarks come a day after the united states and saudi arabia signed a civil nuclear cooperation framework.

Article Title: Jaishankar says attacks on civilian shipping cannot be tolerated, calls for maritime security amid Middle East crisis
Article Text:
Addressing the summit, Jaishankar stressed the need for diplomatic engagement to resolve the ongoing crises in West Asia

Global conflicts affecting economy, Global South: Jaishankar

Holds bilateral meetings with Oman, UK, Netherlands, Philippines

Poll Vote & Share your view Should nations increase cooperation to combat cybercrime as urged by External Affairs Minister Jaishankar? Yes, it is essential for global security No, each nation should manage its own issues 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: External affairs minister S Jaishankar on Thursday said attacks on seafarers, commercial shipping and critical infrastructure "cannot be countenanced under any circumstances", as he called for strict adherence to international maritime law and greater regional cooperation during the 21st East Asia Summit (EAS) in Manila.Addressing the summit, Jaishankar stressed the need for diplomatic engagement to resolve the ongoing crises in Middle East and the Gulf region while reaffirming India's commitment to ensuring safe and unhindered navigation through international sea lanes."Under no circumstances can attacks on seafarers, civilian shipping or infrastructure be countenanced," Jaishankar said.He also reiterated India's support for Asean centrality and an open, free, inclusive and prosperous Indo-Pacific, saying the current global order is marked by uncertainty, strategic rivalry and growing geopolitical tensions.Following the summit, Jaishankar said in a post on X that the global economy was facing the consequences of multiple ongoing conflicts."The world economy is today reeling under the impact of multiple conflicts; energy, food and health security cannot be taken for granted, especially by the Global South," he said.He added that structural changes in global politics and technology were reshaping international relations."There are structural changes being created by new capabilities, exposure and relationships; politics and security increasingly override economics and efficiency while the propensity to take risks and push the envelope is growing," he said.On the South China Sea, Jaishankar reiterated India's support for the early conclusion of a substantive, effective and legally binding Code of Conduct (CoC) that is fully consistent with the 1982 United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) without prejudicing the legitimate rights of countries that are not parties to the negotiations.The minister also reaffirmed India's backing for Asean-led efforts to achieve a peaceful resolution to the crisis in Myanmar.Calling for stronger international cooperation against cybercrime, Jaishankar urged member countries to take coordinated action against transnational cyber scam networks that have trapped several Indian citizens in recent years, as per ANI.On the humanitarian front, he highlighted India's relief and rehabilitation assistance worldwide and said New Delhi had emerged as a leading donor to the United Nations Relief and Works Agency (UNRWA)."We believe that a two-state solution can lead to a lasting and durable peace," he said.Jaishankar also announced that India will host the 7th EAS Conference on Maritime Security Cooperation in Kochi and the EAS Maritime Heritage Festival at Lothal.On the sidelines of the Asean-related Foreign Ministers' Meetings, Jaishankar also held a series of bilateral meetings with leaders from Oman, the United Kingdom, the Netherlands, the Philippines and UNRWA.During his meeting with Oman's foreign minister Sayyid Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi, the two leaders exchanged views on the latest developments in the Gulf region."Appreciated meeting with FM Sayyid Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi of Oman in Manila. Exchanged perspectives on the recent developments in the Gulf region. Agreed to remain in touch," Jaishankar said in a post on X.In his first meeting with newly appointed UK foreign secretary Edward Miliband, Jaishankar congratulated him on his appointment and discussed expanding bilateral ties following the implementation of the recently concluded India-UK Free Trade Agreement."Congratulated him on his appointment. Agreed to build on the positive momentum generated through the recent India-UK FTA implementation, and work towards strengthening our Comprehensive Strategic Partnership," he posted.Jaishankar also met Christian Saunders, acting commissioner-general of UNRWA, and discussed India's humanitarian contributions, including commitments made at the recent donors' conference in the areas of health and capacity building.With Dutch foreign minister Tom Berendsen, Jaishankar reviewed the outcomes of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's recent visit to the Netherlands and discussed implementing the newly elevated India-Netherlands Strategic Partnership."The elevation of our ties to a Strategic Partnership has opened new avenues of cooperation, including in semiconductors, water resources, green hydrogen, health & culture," he said.The external affairs minister also called on Philippine President Ferdinand "Bongbong" Marcos Jr., conveying Prime Minister Narendra Modi's greetings and reaffirming India's support for the Philippines' Asean chairmanship."India-Philippines cooperation under the aegis of our Strategic Partnership continues to diversify as we implement the India-Philippines Plan of Action 2025-2029," Jaishankar said.Jaishankar is on a two-day visit to Manila, where he attended the Asean-India Foreign Ministers' Meeting, the East Asia Summit Foreign Ministers' Meeting, the Asean Regional Forum and the Quad Foreign Ministers' Meeting, where India, the United States, Japan and Australia reaffirmed their commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific and support for Asean centrality.
Article Summary: S jaishankar said attacks on seafarers, commercial shipping and critical infrastructure "cannot be countenanced under any circumstances" he called for strict adherence to international maritime law and greater regional cooperation during the 21st east asia summit (eas) in manila.

Article Title: CJP protest: Passports of people involved in violence may be cancelled, says report
Article Text:
Police vows strict action against those involved in violence

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you support the cancellation of passports for those involved in violent protests? Yes, it's justified No, it's too harsh 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: People found to be directly involved in the violence during the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led protests could face cancellation of their passports, news agency PTI reported citing Delhi Police sources on Thursday.They said investigators are using CCTV footage, video recordings and other technical evidence to identify those who were directly involved in rioting and violent incidents during the protest.The sources added that authorities could initiate proceedings to cancel the passports of individuals found to have actively participated in the violence, in line with the relevant legal provisions.The latest police action follows violence that broke out near Jantar Mantar last night during protests over the NEET paper leak issue.According to Delhi Police, inspector Nand Kishor Singh was injured after he was allegedly attacked by a group of protesters while walking from his parked vehicle to his duty point near Jantar Mantar."There were around 4,000 to 5,000 people gathered there. They were raising anti-police slogans and anti-government slogans. I thought they were students and I was going to perform my duty," PTI quoted Singh saying."They assaulted me with lathis, sticks, stones and whatever sharp objects they had in their hands.I sustained injuries and somehow managed to escape. I was in uniform when they attacked me," he added.The clashes between protesters and police left several police personnel injured, according to the force. Delhi Police has since launched an investigation to identify those allegedly involved in the assault on officers as well as damage to public property, saying action is being taken against those responsible.
Article Summary: Police vows strict action against those involved in violence during the cockroach janta party (cjp)-led protests. Authorities could initiate proceedings to cancel the passports of individuals found to have actively participated in the violence. Police action follows violence that broke out near jantar mantar last night during protests over the neet paper leak issue.

Article Title: Offline maps, LoRa devices and tear gas hacks: Jantar Mantar protest survival tips trend online
Article Text: NEW DELHI: As protests led by the Cockroach Janata Party continued at Jantar Mantar amid repeated clashes with police, social media has been flooded with "survival guides" offering tips on coping with internet shutdowns, tear gas exposure and police action.From downloading offline maps and using Bluetooth-based messaging apps to carrying antacid solutions for tear gas exposure, demonstrators are increasingly relying on crowdsourced advice to navigate the ongoing agitation.One social media user, who said he had been stranded during repeated internet shutdowns at the protest site, advised participants to download offline maps before arriving. He also recommended installing Bluetooth-based messaging applications that function without mobile data or WiFi, enabling communication even during connectivity disruptions.The user further suggested installing locally run large language models (LLMs) that operate directly on devices instead of cloud-based AI services, allowing access to AI tools even when internet services are suspended.He also advised protesters to carry LoRa-based communication devices, which can enable long-range communication when mobile networks are unavailable, and urged them to save the contact details of at least five people living near the protest venue to use in emergencies.Advice on dealing with tear gas has also gained traction online. A social media user recommended carrying a mixture of equal parts water and an antacid such as Maalox or Digene, claiming it could help reduce the burning sensation and eye irritation caused by tear gas exposure. She said she shared the remedy after a friend found it useful during an earlier protest.Another protester said he carried a painter's mask or an ordinary dust mask along with protective goggles to reduce exposure to smoke and chemical irritants.Some demonstrators have also devised makeshift protective gear against baton charges. One protester said he placed thermocol sheets inside his jeans around the back and hip area to provide cushioning. He added that he carried a spare shirt for himself and a kurta for any female protester in case someone's clothes were torn during the demonstration.The protester also said he carried two helmets, one for himself and another to lend to anyone who needed protection.The trend reflects how demonstrators are increasingly turning to social media for practical advice and real-time information during the protests.On Monday, several students and other protesters were injured in clashes with police during their march to Parliament. One woman was rushed to Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital in critical condition. Several police personnel were also injured in the confrontations.The students, protesting under the banner of the Cockroach Janata Party, have been demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET paper leak controversy.
Article Summary: Protesters are increasingly relying on crowdsourced advice to navigate the ongoing agitation. One social media user, who said he had been stranded during repeated internet shutdowns at the protest site, advised participants to download offline maps before arriving.

Article Title: 30,000-ft health scare: British Airways launches probe after 3 pilots suffer food poisoning on Hyderabad-London flight
Article Text:
30,000-ft health scare: British Airways launches probe after 3 pilots suffer food poisoning on Hyderabad-London flight

NEW DELHI: Three British Airways pilots operating a flight from Hyderabad to London fell ill after suspected food poisoning, prompting the airline to launch an investigation into the incident. One of the pilots became incapacitated while the aircraft was cruising at 30,000 feet and required oxygen and urgent medical treatment after landing.The incident occurred on British Airways flight BA276, which departed Hyderabad for London Heathrow after the crew's overnight stopover in the city.According to Gulf News, the three pilots had breakfast together at a hotel lounge in Hyderabad before the flight. However, they reportedly consumed bottled water supplied by an external company.The first pilot became seriously unwell during the flight and was unable to continue normal duties. The other two pilots completed the journey safely, although their condition reportedly worsened as the flight progressed.The aircraft landed safely in London without any reported operational issues.British Airways has confirmed the incident and said it is reviewing the crew's accommodation arrangements in Hyderabad."The welfare of our colleagues is a top priority, and we are reviewing crew accommodation in this location following some isolated reports of sickness," the airline said in a statement.All three pilots have filed Air Safety Reports and were asked to provide medical samples to help doctors identify the source of the illness.The incident is considered highly unusual because commercial airlines follow strict safety protocols designed to prevent all members of the flight deck crew from becoming ill at the same time. These measures often include encouraging or requiring pilots to eat different meals.Food poisoning has long been recognised as a potential aviation safety risk and is one of the reasons airlines maintain strict crew meal policies.British Airways has faced similar incidents in the past. In 1984, a major food poisoning outbreak affected nearly 1,000 passengers, crew members and ground staff, disrupting the airline's global operations. More recently, in 2024, a United Airlines flight from Incheon to San Francisco returned to South Korea after several pilots and cabin crew members became ill shortly after take-off, according to Arabian Business.
Article Summary: Three british airways pilots suffer food poisoning on hyderabad-london flight. One of the pilots became incapacitated while the aircraft was cruising at 30,000 feet. The aircraft landed safely in london without any reported operational issues. British airways has confirmed the incident and said it is reviewing the crew's accommodation arrangements in hyderabad.

Article Title: Parliament standoff: Government reaches out to opposition for NEET debate, urges talks without preconditions
Article Text:
Poll Vote & Share your view What is your stance on the protests led by the opposition parties in Parliament? Support their cause Disagree with their methods 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: The government is reaching out to the opposition leaders to break the deadlock in Parliament over the NEET paper leak issue, government sources told news agency ANI on Thursday. This is a fresh effort from the government's side to end the repeated disruptions that have stalled proceedings through the first week of the Monsoon session.According to government sources, the Centre has conveyed to opposition parties that it is ready for a discussion on the alleged NEET paper leak but wants the debate to take place "without any preconditions", including the opposition's demand for the resignation of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.The government is also likely to convene a meeting of floor leaders soon in an attempt to build consensus on holding the discussion, the sources said.The outreach comes amid a continuing impasse in Parliament, where proceedings have been repeatedly disrupted over the issue.Earlier in the day, Union home minister Amit Shah met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla after the House was adjourned amid sustained protests by opposition members.Before Shah's meeting with the Speaker, leader of the opposition Rahul Gandhi, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav, Trinamool Congress MPs Saugata Roy and Mahua Moitra were among the opposition leaders who met Birla.During Question Hour, the Speaker had asked opposition leaders to meet him to discuss the duration and scheduling of a debate on the NEET paper leak issue.The government has maintained that it is prepared for a discussion but has accused the opposition of insisting on Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation as a condition for participating in the debate. Opposition parties have been demanding accountability over the alleged examination irregularities and have pressed for the minister to step down.The Monsoon session, which began on Monday, has witnessed repeated adjournments as opposition parties protested over the NEET paper leak and the alleged theft of donations from the Ram temple. Police action against Cockroach Janata Party (CJP)-led protests in Delhi has also figured prominently in the opposition's protests inside Parliament.
Article Summary: The government is reaching out to the opposition leaders to break the deadlock in parliament over the neet paper leak issue. This is a fresh effort from the government's side to end the repeated disruptions that have stalled proceedings through the first week of the monsoon session.

Article Title: US strikes Iran for 13th successive night; Trump says Tehran will pay for ship damage
Article Text:
US-Iran hostilities stretch into 13th night

'All damages will be paid for by Iranian money,' says Trump



Iran targets US bases in Gulf



Two weeks after an interim truce effectively collapsed, bringing renewed escalation to the conflict, the United States launched overnight airstrikes on Iran into Thursday, prompting Tehran to retaliate with missile attacks targeting neighbouring Arab countries hosting US military bases.The US carried out another wave of missile strikes late Thursday and early Friday, marking the 13th consecutive night of attacks.Iranian state media reported that missiles struck Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz on both nights.The strategic waterway, now virtually closed to commercial shipping, has emerged as the focal point of the conflict, which is well into its fifth month and has already claimed thousands of lives while fuelling fears of a global economic slowdown.US president Donald Trump on Thursday said any damage to ships and cargo resulting from the ongoing conflict with Iran would be paid for using Iranian assets held under Washington's control."Please let this statement serve to represent, until further notice, that from this point forth, any and all damages done to Ships, Cargo, or anything related thereto, will be paid for by Iranian Money that the United States has in its possession, and controls.These damages may be very substantial but, nevertheless, this is the fair and equitable thing to do. Thank you for your attention to this matter!" Trump said in a post on Truth Social.The latest Truth Social post came hours after Trump on Thursday said that Iran cannot be allowed to possess nuclear weapons, adding that Washington was doing "extremely well" as the conflict between the two countries continued.Iran said it targeted US missile systems, weapons depots and fuel storage facilities in Jordan, along with American military positions in Kuwait, in its latest round of retaliatory strikes.Jordan's military said it intercepted four Iranian missiles and six drones over the past 24 hours, adding that all but one missile were successfully brought down. The remaining missile landed in an uninhabited area, causing no reported casualties.Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed they struck a US electronic warfare unit at the Al-Adiri base in Kuwait, saying the attack caused unspecified casualties. The strike, they said, was in retaliation for overnight US attacks on Iran's Shalamcheh border crossing with Iraq.Iranian military spokesperson Brigadier General Mohammad Akraminia said Tehran would continue its retaliatory operations as long as the United States persisted with attacks on Iranian infrastructure and coastal regions, according to Iranian state television.However, neither the United States nor Kuwaiti authorities have reported any casualties or damage at the base.
Article Summary: Us-iran hostilities stretch into 13th night. Trump says any damage to ships and cargo will be paid for using iranian assets held under washington's control. Iran says it targeted us missile systems, weapons depots and fuel storage facilities in jordan, along with american military positions in kuwait.
